title: China's supreme court bans Infineon from selling GaN power chips in China market-leader Innoscience secures major victory in multi-region patent war
summary: China's Supreme People's Court upheld an injunction against Infineon in China. This blocks Infineon from selling disputed GaN power chips in mainland China. Innoscience secures a major victory in a multi-region patent war.
insight: Patent disputes can drastically impact global semiconductor market share.

title: Cancelled Xbox 360 version of GoldenEye 007 gets recompiled for PC No emulator, the game runs as a real native executable, insists dev
summary: GoldenEye 007 has been recompiled into a native PC executable, providing a native port of the classic game without using emulation.
insight: Native PC port preserves original game performance and stability.

title: Cooler Master MasterHUB review: A modular stream deck with potential
summary: The Cooler Master MasterHUB is a modular, customizable macropad with potential, but its software and plugin limitations hold it back. Its modularity is a highlight, but it falls short in terms of customization options. The MasterHUB could be a great tool for streamers and gamers, but it needs improvement.
insight: Modularity is only as good as the software that supports it.

title: Score 32GB of DDR5 RAM from only $240 in these Newegg hardware bundles for Intel and AMD gaming PC builds huge savings on premium Gigabyte motherboards coupled with popular Corsair Vengeance memory
summary: Newegg offers bundles with 32GB DDR5 RAM and Gigabyte motherboards starting at $240. These affordable bundles cater to Intel and AMD gaming PC builds, combining premium components at a discounted price.
insight: Budget-friendly gaming PC builds with premium components now within reach.

title: Asus ProArt PA27USD 27-inch OLED review: Precision color with high-speed gaming prowess
summary: Asus ProArt PA27USD offers professional-grade color accuracy and high-speed gaming features in a 27-inch QD-OLED display.
insight: Blurs line between professional and gaming displays with impressive specs.

title: Google Chromebook marks its 15th anniversary slow feature rollouts and a canceled Steam beta leave it largely stuck in classrooms
summary: Google Chromebook marks its 15th anniversary amidst slow feature rollouts and limited adoption outside classrooms. Despite its initial promise, Chromebooks have failed to gain significant traction in the consumer market. The platform's evolution has been hindered by slow feature updates and canceled projects, such as the Steam beta.
insight: Stagnant Chromebook development limits its appeal beyond education

title: Samsung's 49-inch ultrawide Odyssey G9 gaming monitor dips to the lowest-ever price of $664 at Amazon get 240Hz refresh rate and dense 109 PPI for 34% off
summary: Save 34% on Samsung's 49-inch Odyssey G9 gaming monitor, now at $664.99, its lowest-ever price. This massive display offers a 240Hz refresh rate and 109 PPI.
insight: Affordable high-end gaming monitor option for serious gamers

title: FBI dismantles Chinese phishing service that coached buyers to generate scam sites using AI $88 cybercrime product linked to $1.9 billion in losses, 3.87 million stolen cards
summary: The FBI, Google, and Lumen Technologies dismantled China-based Outsider Enterprise phishing service, which coached buyers to generate scam sites using AI and an $88 cybercrime product. This operation is linked to $1.9 billion in losses and 3.87 million stolen cards.
insight: Advanced AI-powered phishing services amplify cybercrime and financial losses exponentially.

title: 42% slashed off Samsung's 990 Pro SSD, 2TB now $369 $270 savings brings one of the fastest PCIe 4.0 SSDs to its lowest price in months
summary: Samsung's 990 Pro 2TB SSD offers excellent performance and efficiency at a lower price point.
insight: Fast PCIe 4.0 storage now more affordable for consumers.

title: 2021 Honda Civic infotainment system can be jailbroken via USB flaw uses public Android test keys to install unauthorized apps, enables for 'EvilValet' attacks
summary: A security flaw in the 2021 Honda Civic infotainment system allows unauthorized app installation through a USB port, using publicly known Android test keys. This enables potential 'EvilValet' attacks, where malicious updates can be loaded with malware. The vulnerability is due to the system's reliance on a publicly known AOSP test key.
insight: Publicly known keys compromise Android-based infotainment system security

title: Intel's upcoming 'Raptor Lake Next' will reportedly top out at 20 cores and retain Core 200 branding Lineup may include a special 10-core SKU with 24MB of L3 cache
summary: Intel's rumored 'Raptor Lake Next' might feature up to 20 cores and Core 200 branding, potentially offering a budget-friendly option alongside Nova Lake.
insight: Boosting multi-threading capabilities, targeting budget-oriented market needs and power efficiency

title: Amazon says its data centers consume only 0.075% of the water Americans use for watering their lawns and gardens company also boasts of its improvements in water efficiency
summary: Amazon's data centers use 0.075% of U.S. water used for lawns and gardens annually. The company consumes 2.5 billion gallons of water yearly for data center cooling. This is a fraction of the 3.3 trillion gallons used for watering lawns and gardens.
insight: Water usage for data centers is a tiny fraction of lawn watering.

title: Computer History Museum recalls astonishing retro haul recovered from abandoned German warehouse over 2,000 artifacts spanning the 1930s to 1980s required seven tractor-trailers after a WWII bomb scare
summary: The Computer History Museum recovered over 2,000 retro artifacts from a German warehouse. The collection spans the 1930s to 1980s, requiring seven tractor-trailers. The rescue was complicated by a WWII bomb scare.
insight: Preserving computing history helps understand technological advancements and innovation.

title: Apple made marketing gold from the export ban on Power Mac G4 'supercomputer' in 1999, 'for the first time in history a personal computer has been classified as a weapon' Pentagon banned sales of the 400 MHz G4 in 50 countries when it launched
summary: In 1999, Apple's PowerMac G4 was banned in 50 countries by the Pentagon, but Steve Jobs turned the situation into a marketing opportunity, showcasing the computer's capabilities and fueling its appeal. This event is often cited as an example of successful crisis management and innovative marketing.
insight: Crisis can be opportunity for innovative marketing and branding.

title: Researchers recycle old phones and cluster them into computing platforms that operate as a low-cost data center says processors on modern smartphones deliver higher single-core performance than comparable multicore servers
summary: Researchers from UC San Diego repurposed old smartphones as a low-cost data center, leveraging their processing power to run apps locally.
insight: Old smartphones can deliver comparable performance to modern servers inexpensively.

title: Snapmaker launches $150,000 Innovation Fund for open source 3D printing cash rewards target developers backing the U1 toolchanger across Klipper, OrcaSlicer, and Moonraker ecosystems
summary: Snapmaker launches a $150,000 Innovation Fund to support open-source 3D printing developers. The fund targets rewards for developers working on the U1 toolchanger in Klipper, OrcaSlicer, and Moonraker ecosystems. This initiative celebrates Snapmaker's 10th anniversary and fosters collaboration in the 3D printing community.
insight: Boosting open-source innovation with $150,000 cash rewards and ecosystem integration.

title: New 3D printer tech uses elliptical laser beams to stir molten metal and create alloys-on-demand existing machinery can implement technique in software meaning for more convenient, stronger alloy printing
summary: NIST has demonstrated a 3D printing method that uses elliptical laser paths to stir molten metal, creating alloys on-demand. This technique can be implemented in existing machinery through software updates, making it more convenient and accessible. The result is stronger alloy printing with improved properties.
insight: Convenience and strength through software-upgradeable alloy printing technology emerges.

title: Microsoft is reportedly testing Copilot+ AI features with discrete GPUs instead of NPUs a feature available on Windows App SDK with a Windows Insider Experimental Channel build and Developer Mode turned on
summary: Microsoft is testing Copilot+ AI features on discrete GPUs, expanding user access to Windows 11 local AI capabilities. This move allows more users to utilize AI features on non-Copilot devices. The shift is part of the Windows App SDK.
insight: Expanding AI capabilities to a broader range of hardware

title: OpenAI hit with sweeping probe from massive coalition of 42 US state attorneys general just days after reported IPO filing subpoena targets ChatGPT maker s ads, data practices, handling of minors, model sycophancy, and safety policies
summary: 42 US state attorneys general launched a probe into OpenAI, seeking documents on its advertising, data handling, and safety practices, including how it handles minors and models. This investigation comes days after OpenAI reportedly filed for an initial public offering. The subpoena targets various aspects of OpenAI's operations.
insight: OpenAI's practices are under scrutiny as a major AI player goes public.

title: US government warned Anthropic that Fable 5 had been jailbroken, but firm 'refused' to fix before US implemented export controls Anthropic defended its decision by saying the jailbreak 'isn t serious,' Chinese group had reportedly accessed model
summary: The US government warned Anthropic about a Fable 5 jailbreak, but the company refused to fix it before US export controls were implemented, reportedly downplaying the vulnerability's severity.
insight: US export controls may prevent tech firms from mitigating security risks.

title: AI cryptomining network's 320,000 RTX 3090-class GPUs allegedly burn 112 megawatts of power on zero useful AI computation GPU rental costs jump 38%, but Pearl s cards are doing random matrix math, study claims
summary: Pearl's AI mining network allegedly consumes 112 MW of power without producing verified useful AI computation, causing a 38% jump in GPU rental costs.
insight: Massive energy wastage undermines AI's efficiency claims and environmental benefits

title: Linux 7.1 Release Main changes, Arm, RISC-V, and MIPS architectures
summary: Linux 7.1 has been released, marking a new version of the popular operating system. The release includes various improvements and changes, particularly focusing on Arm, RISC-V, and MIPS architectures. This update is expected to bring enhanced performance and stability to users of these architectures.
insight: Enhanced performance and stability for Arm, RISC-V, and MIPS users

title: Gateworks Catalina GW9200 NXP i.MX 95 SBC features Flexible Socket Adapter sockets for M.2 or mPCIe modules
summary: Gateworks' Catalina GW9200 SBC features NXP i.MX 95 SoC with Flexible Socket Adapter (FSA) sockets for M.2 or mini PCIe expansion, supporting industrial Edge AI applications with 10GbE and GbE connectivity.
insight: Supports Edge AI and industrial applications with flexible expansion options.

title: Best of Sensors: MethaneTrack brings realtime gas leak detection
summary: NevadaNano's MethaneTrack system won a Best of Sensors award for its real-time gas leak detection capabilities.
insight: Real-time gas leak detection saves lives and reduces environmental impact immediately.

title: Best of Sensors: InPlay's NanoBeacon targets temperature sensing
summary: InPlay received a Best of Sensors 2026 award for their SoC addressing temperature sensing challenges. Their NanoBeacon SoC offers innovative solutions for temperature measurement and monitoring. This technology improves accuracy and reliability in various applications.
insight: Precise temperature sensing enables better decision-making in critical systems.

title: Bitcoin is down nearly 50% and the debate over its value is heating up again
summary: Bitcoin's value is experiencing significant volatility, prompting investors to reassess its worth. Some are taking profits as interest rates remain high, while others see opportunities in emerging high-growth themes. This highlights the inherent risks of speculative investments.
insight: Volatility in Bitcoin's value poses significant risks for investors.

title: Researchers are turning old Pixel phones into a data center and they outperform some server hardware
summary: Researchers repurpose old Pixel phones into a data center, outperforming some server hardware. This reduces embodied carbon emissions and e-waste. The project extends the life of discarded devices.
insight: Reducing e-waste through creative repurposing reduces carbon emissions.

title: How often do the sensors inside the 2026 FIFA World Cup ball record data?
summary: The 2026 FIFA World Cup ball features advanced sensors that track its movement in real-time, providing detailed data on speed, spin, and other parameters to enhance the fan experience and improve gameplay analysis.
insight: Advanced sensors enhance gameplay analysis and fan experience precision.

title: SK Hynix moves 375-layer NAND into mass production, replacing tungsten with molybdenum
summary: SK Hynix has moved into mass production of 375-layer NAND flash memory, replacing tungsten with molybdenum. Production is taking place in existing fabs, previously used for 321-layer V9 NAND. The 375-layer design is internally referred to as a '400-layer' product.
insight: Breakthrough in NAND density boosts storage capacity and performance significantly.

title: A $200 ChatGPT subscription could cost OpenAI $14,000 if you actually used it to its full potential
summary: SemiAnalysis calculated the theoretical maximum cost of OpenAI subscriptions based on usage limits. They found a significant gap between actual and theoretical costs, with a $200 subscription potentially costing $14,000 at standard API rates. This highlights the potential for large cost savings with more efficient usage.
insight: Theoretical maximum usage exposes vast cost gaps in AI subscription models.

title: Netgear countersuit says TP-Link's American company rebrand is false advertising
summary: Netgear countersued TP-Link over rebranding of American company as false advertising.
insight: TP-Link's rebranding faces scrutiny over authenticity and marketing claims.

title: Intel could launch 'Raptor Lake Next' in 2027 as DDR4 makes a comeback
summary: Intel may launch 'Raptor Lake Next' in 2027 alongside older DDR4 architecture.
insight: Intel is driven by platform realities over standard product cycles.

title: AMD mocks MacBook Neo's PC gaming support, says it runs only 5 of the top 20 titles natively
summary: AMD claims the MacBook Neo can only run 5 of the top 20 PC games natively.
insight: AMD challenges MacBook Neo's PC gaming capabilities on performance grounds.
